findings.title: Notification Priority Levels Implementation
findings.description: Implementing a three-tier notification priority system requires changes to the notification rendering logic and user interface components. This will also necessitate updates to the backend to support priority levels.
assumptions: The current notification system can be modified to include priority levels.

evidence.excerpt: Propose a three-tier priority system that visually differentiates urgency.

recommendations: Develop a detailed design for the priority levels and integrate with the existing notification system.

findings.title: Push Notification Delivery Failure on Android 14
findings.description: The issue with push notifications failing on Android 14 devices poses a significant risk to user engagement and satisfaction. This needs immediate attention to ensure reliable notification delivery.
assumptions: The issue is isolated to Android 14 and can be fixed with a software update.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.

recommendations: Investigate the root cause of the delivery failure and implement a fix as soon as possible.

findings.title: ML-based Notification Batching
findings.description: Implementing ML-based notification batching will require a robust ML model that can learn user preferences. This involves data collection, model training, and integration into the notification system.
assumptions: The existing infrastructure can support ML model integration.

evidence.excerpt: Batch low-priority notifications into a digest delivered at optimal times.

recommendations: Evaluate existing ML frameworks and consider building or buying a solution for user preference learning.

findings.title: User Preference Page for Notification Categories
findings.description: Creating a user preference page requires a new API endpoint and a React component. This will depend on the backend team's ability to deliver the API in a timely manner.
assumptions: The backend team can prioritize the development of the new API.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

recommendations: Coordinate with the backend team to ensure timely delivery of the API.

findings.title: Notification Delivery Latency Reduction
findings.description: Reducing notification delivery latency from p95 340ms to under 200ms is a complex task that involves architectural changes to the delivery pipeline. This poses a risk of introducing new issues during implementation.
assumptions: The proposed architectural changes will effectively reduce latency.

evidence.excerpt: Root cause appears to be synchronous database writes in the delivery pipeline.

recommendations: Conduct thorough testing of the new architecture before deployment.

summary: The analysis identifies critical dependencies and risks associated with the SmartNotify project. Key components include implementing notification priority levels, addressing push notification failures on Android 14, and enhancing user preferences. The phased delivery plan suggests focusing on MVP for immediate fixes and V1 for feature enhancements, while V2 addresses performance improvements.
